title: Im just going to say it Rebuild is bad
display_title: Im just going to say it Rebuild is bad
wikitext:
[]: I'm just going to say it... Rebuild is bad [SPOILERS] Post by sedemihcrA » Wed Oct 28, 2009 7:57 pm That's right. It's bad. In that mediocre, popcorn-eating, probably gunna go see it anyways but eh, stereotypical summer blockbuster movie way. That kind of bad. And I'll tell you exactly why. It's not the semi-pointless changes. It's not who's getting paired with who this time around or which characters are acting this way instead of that way . It's not even the ridiculously obfuscated plotlines... rant (key of Nebuchanezzer... uh okay, that's Adam-y enough I suppose... oh, wait here's some random girl who happens to be a secret agent for a mysterious someone that's never identified, oh and she's fourteen because that's, you know, like totally believable... all right... oh and actually she knows a secret code which activates "ultra-mode" in Evangelions just by saying some stupid phrase out loud but which no one else seems to know including the pilots and oh by the way did we mention this is not the "true evangelion" because that requires some other kinda whatsit inserted into the googah to absorb the human from the blablabla.... great. Awesome job. It's so much simpler now. I expect that kinda crap in my 80s mecha, or in delicious parody of it, Gurren Lagann-style, sure, but seriously? A cheat code for Evas? Is this the 21st century? Surely we've progressed from the pilots narrating every attack as they perform it, that old-school Mazinger Z crapola...) /rant No, the reason it's bad is very simple: You can't carve up a couple of TV episodes, glue them together and call it a movie. Unless you want a badly paced, wildly uneven, completely blah-experience. Then I suppose you can call it a movie. You can call it Rebuild 2.0 if you add in about a bajillion special effects shots so that your eyeballs are roasted so hard you forget you don't give a shit about any of these people by the end of the ninety minutes. Watching 2.0 was like watching plasticized 2D versions ( ha ha ) of some of my favorite fictional characters run jerkily into each other in some terrible parody of what we call characterization. They weren't even the same characters anymore, they were just these creepy clones, lookalikes with all of the heart of about one dead walrus. I put it to all of you: Did any of this movie even make you feel an ounce, the tiniest fraction, a bare snippet of the feelings you experienced watching the series? For myself, the answer is a definitive no . You know how I know? It was easy actually. Anno, kind author that he is, gave us a near-perfect 1-to-1 with the series, an emotional litmus test... because he gave us the scenes with Bardiel a second time around. In the series, that was probably one of the most heart-wrenching bits for me. The end of the episode where Shinji finally sees near-dead Touji just blew me away. I was in utter shock. I still get chills every time I re-watch the series up to this point. In Rebuild... it's just absurd. We get all of this violence, all the way to the exaggerated chomping of the Entry Plug (yes, doing everything it can to outdo the same sequence on TV, four arms instead of two, more entrails, you name it.) And we reach this supposedly awful point with Asuka in the Entry Plug! My favorite character! Shinji screaming to make it stop. And you know what? I didn't feel a thing. Not a damn thing. Perhaps for some of you the thrill is enough. The shiny pictures, the flashy animation. I'll grant you the movie was visually breathtaking, no doubt about that. But the soul? It's ironic isn't it? The one element necessary for the operation of the Evangelions themselves, the central key to the original plotline - the soul, my friends, is missing.

[]: Re: I'm just going to say it... Rebuild is bad [SPOILERS] Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:13 pm Oh goody! Another Rebuild hate thread. sedemihcrA wrote: rant ...wait here's some random girl who happens to be a secret agent for a mysterious someone that's never identified, oh and she's fourteen because that's, you know, like totally believable... all right... oh and actually she knows a secret code which activates "ultra-mode" in Evangelions just by saying some stupid phrase out loud but which no one else seems to know including the pilots... A cheat code for Evas? /rant

[]: Re: I'm just going to say it... Rebuild is bad [SPOILERS] Post by UltraBlackPilot » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:13 pm In the series, that was probably one of the most heart-wrenching bits for me. The end of the episode where Shinji finally sees near-dead Touji just blew me away. I was in utter shock. I still get chills every time I re-watch the series up to this point.

[]: Post by CyberXIII » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:16 pm I agree with the above poster's avatar. Rebuild isn't supposed to give you the same feelings as the original series. It's an alternate continuity with entirely new plotlines.

[]: Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:18 pm I think it was stated by one of the directors/producers in NewtypeUSA that this wasnt supposed to be NGE but an Evangelion that can be enjoyed by noobs ands hardcore fans alike. It wasnt meant to be a theatrical version of the original.

[]: Re: I'm just going to say it... Rebuild is bad [SPOILERS] Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:18 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Did any of this movie even make you feel an ounce, the tiniest fraction, a bare snippet of the feelings you experienced watching the series?

[]: Post by sedemihcrA » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:19 pm You didn't feel a thing for Asuka because you already knew how it was gonna end, and because shes not Shinji's best freind.

[]: Post by UltraBlackPilot » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:21 pm sedemihcrA wrote: You didn't feel a thing for Asuka because you already knew how it was gonna end, and because shes not Shinji's best freind.

[]: Post by sedemihcrA » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:27 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: Note:( Whats cadence mean?)

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:29 pm I'm curious. Have you ever seen Death and Rebirth and if you did, did you enjoy it?

[]: Re: I'm just going to say it... Rebuild is bad [SPOILERS] Post by sedemihcrA » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:35 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Oh goody! Another Rebuild hate thread.

[]: Post by sedemihcrA » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:38 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: I'm curious. Have you ever seen Death and Rebirth and if you did, did you enjoy it?

[]: Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:51 pm I wasn't impressed except by the shiny new visuals. The whole thing came across to me as bad fanfiction... switch characters around without changing events, totally fuck up the characterizations, crank up the action and fanservice to 11 even when it makes no sense whatsoever, and maybe even toss in a Mari-sue. For me the killer was Zeruel. Shinji vs. Zeruel was my favorite moment in the original series and this movie managed to completely fuck it up in just about every way I can immagine. I thought I was watching some sort of crossover between Eva, FLCL, and GL by the end, as ridiculously over-the-top it became. No, don't tell me I have to wait for the next one to retroactively make it good. Movies, even middle portions of series, should still be able to stand more or less on their own. This is a critical difference in media between movies and series, and thus 2.0 is an utter fail on the scale of Matrix 2.

[]: Re: I'm just going to say it... Rebuild is bad [SPOILERS] Post by Rock-Slash » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:54 pm sedemihcrA wrote: But the soul? It's ironic isn't it? The one element necessary for the operation of the Evangelions themselves, the central key to the original plotline - the soul, my friends, is missing.

[]: Re: I'm just going to say it... Rebuild is bad [SPOILERS] Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Oct 28, 2009 9:05 pm sedemihcrA wrote: Joseph the PRPD wrote: Oh goody! Another Rebuild hate thread.

[]: Post by Rodeo » Wed Oct 28, 2009 9:07 pm 1.0 was bad but 2.0 completely redeemed it. To me, the emotion and the creative spark was totally there. Much to my surprise, they actually seem to be quite heartfelt about this project. I will grant you that it sounds pretty darn bad on paper.

[]: Post by sedemihcrA » Wed Oct 28, 2009 9:08 pm It's ironic, isn't it NemZ? That's probably the most animation-heavy part movie and yet the whole experience feels dry. Compare it to the long series of stills we sometimes get in Eva, when the characters are often at their most fragile psychologically. Rei's self-destruction sequence. Shinji inside Leliel. Touji staring at the phone when it's raining in Ep. 3, unable to bring himself to call Shinji. Asuka in the bathtub. All of the shiny sparkly goodness doesn't even begin to compare to the feeling conjured out of those stillnesses, those stark moments when the actors (or sometimes just the silence and the framing alone!) really start performing their hearts out because they have to carry the entire scene on their shoulders. That's when everything comes alive. This was my secret fear all along, too. Because of course those scenes only played that way due to the budget limitations in the original. Anno had to be super-clever in using a lot of stillness in Eva because his action sequences were so animation-heavy. But the contrast between them was often what made the action sequences so much more impressive because the whole rhythm of the production picked up with the pace of the episode. A perfect synch ratio. My fear was always that if you get this giant budget going, suddenly those slow, harrowing scenes are smashed out of existence in exchange for some googly-eye-inducing special effects. Sure enough, it happened. If you look at Anno's pacing in this movie compared to any episode, or even EoE, it's atrocious. Anno's so freaking good that it still looks pretty and stays engaging but the weightiness of everything is just totally lost. The movie barely gives itself a chance to breathe. And even then it usually interrupts that with something slapstick. If he ever goes back to doing animation series, I hope he returns to that style again. It was one of the most subtle and powerful things in Eva and Rebuild feels hollow without it.

[]: Post by Lucretius » Wed Oct 28, 2009 9:08 pm I didn't hate Rebuild, but I was frequently struck by the depressing thought that even I could've done a better job adapting the same material. The synopses are better than the actual film.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Wed Oct 28, 2009 9:27 pm Wesa gonna merge this thread or what?

[]: Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 28, 2009 9:30 pm sedemihcrA wrote: That's probably the most animation-heavy part movie and yet the whole experience feels dry.

Article: Im just going to say it Rebuild is bad
, too. Because of course those scenes only played that way due to the budget limitations in the original. Anno had to be super-clever in using a lot of stillness in Eva because his action sequences were so animation-heavy. But the contrast between them was often what made the action sequences so much more impressive because the whole rhythm of the production picked up with the pace of the episode. A perfect synch ratio. My fear was always that if you get this giant budget going, suddenly those slow, harrowing scenes are smashed out of existence in exchange for some googly-eye-inducing special effects. Sure enough, it happened. If you look at Anno's pacing in this movie compared to any episode, or even EoE, it's atrocious. Anno's so freaking good that it still looks pretty and stays engaging but the weightiness of everything is just totally lost. The movie barely gives itself a chance to breathe. And even then it usually interrupts that with something slapstick. If he ever goes back to doing animation series, I hope he returns to that style again. It was one of the most subtle and powerful things in Eva and Rebuild feels hollow without it.
